Repo duplicate checks against mockups.json, master-send-board.json, and recent July 2026 daily batches

publicListingSignals: no website field found in public listing pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: High-intent plumbing searches appear to land on a listing-only path with phone/address but no owned website field found. The nearest leak is lack of a controlled mobile tap-to-call page that confirms location and routes issue types without relying entirely on directory context.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Starter plumbing call-page + Google/local profile website-link cleanup; expand only after owner confirms services, hours, licenses, and photos.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Simple pain, easy proof, and a no-obligation sample page make this low-friction: the value is not a redesign argument, it is finishing the public website button/customer path.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Cream/blue plumbing blueprint and service-ticket concept; phone-first instead of dark contractor hero/right quote form/stat strip.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser/vision QA completed locally. Initial desktop sticky call bar obscured the hero and was removed for desktop; revised above-fold layout is clear, polished, differentiated, and claim-safe with visible sample-site guardrail.

draftCopy.script: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. Quick note for whoever handles the website or Google listing for Fitchburg Plumbing: I was checking local plumbing listings and found a phone/address for you, but I did not find an owned website link in the public listing I reviewed. I made a small no-obligation sample call page showing how that first tap could look: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/fitchburg-plumbing-inc/ — is it worth polishing this up with your real services and photos? If not, no worries; reply or say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: no website field found in public listing pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: Transmission/auto repair has high call intent, but the public listing pass found phone/address without an owned website field. The nearest money leak is searchers not seeing a controlled service page for transmission symptoms, diagnostics, and call prep.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Transmission-specific starter landing page with tap-to-call, symptom checklist, and approved service list.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: A specialty angle is stronger than a generic mechanic homepage; a small transmission call page can be sold as a practical first step, not a full brand overhaul.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created today to avoid overproducing similar auto repair pages; would need a service-specific transmission visual direction before outreach.

draftCopy.script: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking Madison auto repair listings and noticed Badger Transmission has a public phone/address path, but I did not find an owned website link in the listing pass I reviewed. For a transmission shop, I think a small symptom-focused call page could help people know what to say before they call. Worth mocking that up for you? Reply/say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: website fetch failed with SSL certificate hostname mismatch
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The website URL in the listing failed HTTPS validation during the batch fetch because the certificate hostname did not match blackhawkcustomswaterloo.com. That can scare off customers before they ever see the shop.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful SSL/domain alert first; if they want help, fix certificate/canonical domain and then add a simple services/gallery/contact page.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: This is not subjective design criticism; it is a concrete public-path bug with immediate trust impact and an easy first conversation.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Mockup intentionally skipped; a bug alert is more useful than design-first outreach until the domain/certificate path is confirmed/fixed.

draftCopy.subject: Quick heads-up on your website link
draftCopy.body: Hi Black Hawk Customs team — quick helpful note, not a sales blast. While checking Waterloo auto service listings, I tried opening the website listed for Black Hawk Customs and hit an SSL/certificate hostname mismatch for blackhawkcustomswaterloo.com. Customers may see a browser security warning before they reach you. If you already know, ignore me; if not, it may be worth having whoever manages the domain/certificate take a look. I can send the screenshot/details if useful. Reply NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.diagnosis: The site loads and has phone paths, so this is not a rebuild lead. The nearest revenue leak is likely paid/storm traffic readiness: first-page fetch did not expose a simple form, and storm/roof repair campaigns need dedicated landing pages and measurable conversion actions.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Storm-damage/roof-repair landing page with call tracking-ready layout, short intake form, insurance-document/photo checklist, and dedicated campaign copy.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: A focused page complements an existing site rather than insulting it, and roofing has seasonal urgency plus high-ticket economics.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Mockup skipped today; useful version should be a campaign-specific storm landing page using approved brand assets, not a generic roofer redesign.

draftCopy.subject: Davenport roofing landing page idea
draftCopy.body: Hi Kendrick — I was looking at Quad Cities roofing search paths and noticed Burrage already has a live site and phone path, so this is not a “you need a new website” note. The opportunity I saw is more specific: a storm/roof-repair landing page built for ads or seasonal traffic, with a short intake form, call tracking-ready CTA, and photo/insurance checklist. If useful, I can mock up one focused page using only approved Burrage copy/assets. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: no website field found in public listing pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: A tinting/appearance shop is visual by nature, but the public listing pass found phone/address/hours with no website field. The nearest leak is profile-to-gallery conversion: customers may need proof/photos/packages before they call.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: One-page visual profile/gallery starter with package categories to confirm, tap-to-call, hours, and profile website-link cleanup.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The offer is concrete and visual: make the listing feel complete with photos/packages and a simple call path, not a broad web redesign.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Mockup skipped because a high-quality tinting concept needs real photos or approved photo direction; outreach should ask permission before building around visual proof.

draftCopy.script: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I noticed Vivid Window Tinting has phone, address, and weekday hours in the public listing I reviewed, but I did not find an owned website link there. Since tinting is so visual, a simple gallery/package page connected from the listing could make the first call easier. Worth mocking up a small concept with approved photos/packages? Reply/say NO and I will not follow up.

mockupsSkipped.reason: Avoided another generic auto page; needs service-specific transmission direction.
